title: How I'm using AI to read 10x more papers
post:
Literature reviews used to take me weeks. Now I use AI to help me process academic papers at a scale that would have been impossible before — not by replacing my critical analysis, but by handling the first pass.

My workflow: I feed papers into a specialized AI that can discuss the methodology, results, and implications. It surfaces patterns and connections I might miss reading linearly. Then I go deeper on what's actually relevant. I'm reading more thoroughly, not less — I'm just spending my time on the right papers.

This isn't cheating. It's using every tool available to do better research. The researchers who figure this out will have an enormous advantage.

How are you using AI in your research workflow? Thoughts?
